commit.message:
fix: header back to full width, welcome content fixed at viewport center

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: harden verdict parsing against output injection (#28)

* feat: add has_verdict_line() utility for line-exact marker matching

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use line-exact matching for TESTS_PASSING verdict in dev_loop

Replace fragile substring 'in' check with has_verdict_line() to prevent
false positives when the marker appears embedded in prose or diffs.

Addresses finding 3 from #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use verdict constants in tests instead of bare strings

Migrate all test files from bare marker strings to imported VERDICT_*
constants. This ensures tests automatically follow constant value changes.

GitHub API response strings (test_pr_status, test_comments) are left
unchanged since they're not our verdict protocol.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: harden verdict markers with distinctive <<<VERDICT:>>> prefix

Replace bare markers (APPROVED, NEEDS_REVISION, etc.) with bracketed
format (<<<VERDICT:APPROVED>>>) to prevent output injection attacks.
The distinctive format cannot appear naturally in git diffs, echoed user
input, or other untrusted data that agents may include in their output.

Update all agent system prompts to emit the new format.

Addresses findings 1 and 2 from #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use verdict constants in build_review_prompt()

Replace hardcoded bare marker names in the review prompt with the
actual VERDICT_* constants so reviewers see the correct format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fence untrusted issue data in LLM prompts

Wrap issue body with <untrusted> XML tags in all prompt builders
(dispatcher._build_prompt, plan_loop.build_architect_prompt,
dev_loop.run_agent_team) so agents can distinguish trusted instructions
from user-provided data that may contain prompt injection payloads.

Addresses finding 4 from #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add injection resistance regression tests

Verify that bare verdict strings in untrusted data (git diffs, echoed
user input) do not spoof workflow verdicts after marker hardening.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Escape </untrusted> in fence content via HTML entity encoding
- Sanitize fence label to alphanumeric/hyphen/underscore chars
- Fence issue.title in all prompt builders (dev_loop, dispatcher, plan_loop)
- Remove redundant APPROVED_MARKERS assertion loop in tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Initial release: AZSploitMapper v0.2.0

Azure Attack Path Visualizer & CSPM tool with:
- Resource discovery (VMs, NSGs, Storage, Key Vault, Identity, RBAC)
- YAML-based misconfiguration detection (17+ rules)
- Secret scanning (regex-based credential detection)
- Attack graph visualization (Cytoscape.js)
- CIS Azure Benchmark v2.1.0 + NIST SP 800-53 compliance
- API Key auth (single-user auto-login) + Entra ID OAuth2 (multi-user)
- Service Principal authentication (no az login)
- TLS/HTTPS, CSP headers, rate limiting, audit logging
- Docker deployment (non-root, read-only FS, cap_drop ALL)
- Terraform deployment (VNet, NSG, ACI, Managed Identity)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

platform sync. Repositioned as "Sync Now" button inside the Connected

heading and misleading signal processing description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Pass approved plan to coding session via issue comment marker (#289)

The coding session was only receiving the issue title and body, completely
losing the approved plan that was posted as an issue comment during planning.
This caused Claude to code from scratch, ignoring the plan.

Fix: tag plan comments with <!-- erg:plan --> marker during planning, then
fetch issue comments at coding time and include the most recent plan in
the initial message.

commit.message:
Restrict plan approvals to repository collaborators only (#287)

For GitHub-sourced issues, only users who are repository collaborators
(with explicit write access) may approve plans. This applies to:

- gate.approved with comment_match trigger: comments from
  non-collaborators are skipped even if they match the pattern
- plan.user_replied: non-collaborator comments still fire the event
  (feedback is captured) but plan_approved is forced to false

Collaborator status is checked via the GitHub API:
  GET /repos/:owner/:repo/collaborators/{username}
Non-GitHub issue sources (Asana, Linear) are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v1.3.5: extract onboarding-coach.ts + analytics instrumentation

Refactor:
- Extract src/onboarding-coach.ts (313 lines) from content.ts — shadow DOM
  coach, playground beacon, CHECK_ONBOARDING listener. Dependency injection
  (initOnboarding({ toggleCapture })) avoids circular import. Vite bundles
  into same content.js — no manifest/injection impact.
- content.ts reduced 2,202 → 1,926 lines.

Analytics:
- capture_cancelled: add stage (selector_open|preview_shown) + method
  (button|escape) params. New GA4 fire for Escape in selector mode
  (previously untracked). Remove alert() on cancel (silent UX).
- refresh_completed: add domains param (sorted unique hostnames, loop-safe
  GA4 100-char limit).

commit.message:
Fix heat layer visibility for cities with fewer data points

The heat layer was invisible for sparser cities (Denver 68pts, Nashville
126pts, Miami 423pts) because maxZoom:15 suppressed intensity at viewing
zoom 10-11, and the gradient's first color matched the dark map background.
Fix: dynamic maxZoom relative to city zoom, larger radius, minOpacity,
and visible gradient at low intensities.

fix(types): convert all supabase.from() calls to fromTable() for CI typecheck

CI runs tsc with tsconfig.app.json which is stricter than the default
tsconfig. Tables not in the auto-generated Database type cause TS2769
overload errors. This converts all remaining supabase.from() calls for
untyped tables across 10 hook files to use the fromTable() helper that
centralizes the type cast.

commit.message:
Fix/initial citation highlight (#9)

* Fix initial citation highlight on first pane open

Root cause: React StrictMode double-invokes PdfHighlighter.init(), causing
the pdfjs viewer to stay wired to eventBus1 while listeners move to eventBus2.
textlayerrendered fires on the wrong bus so renderHighlightLayers() is never
called, and findOrCreateHighlightLayer() returns null because the target page's
textLayer hasn't been rendered yet.

Fix: defer activeCitation on first open until (1) pdfjs is initialised,
(2) we scroll to the target page so pdfjs renders it, (3) the target page's
.textLayer element exists. At that point highlights arrives as a prop change,
componentDidUpdate fires, renderHighlightLayers() finds a valid textLayer,
and the highlight renders correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use yellow highlight colour instead of red for active citation

Override the library's scrolled-to highlight colour (#ff4141) with yellow
(rgba(255,212,0,0.55)) to match the standard PDF annotation look.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split testing into 18 dedicated phases — one vuln type per phase

Old Phase 9 "Advanced Web Tests" mixed 10+ vuln types in one phase.
Now each vulnerability gets its own dedicated phase with systematic
iteration over ALL forms and ALL parameters:

Phase 1-4:   Recon, Headers, Auth, Session
Phase 5-7:   XSS, SQLi, CSRF (each iterates ALL forms)
Phase 8-9:   Tech fingerprinting, CORS/Redirect/SSL
Phase 10-14: CMDi, SSTI, SSRF, Deserialization, File Upload
Phase 15-16: GraphQL, Protocol attacks
Phase 17:    IDOR (2-account)
Phase 18:    Final report

No more chaining multiple bug types in one phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: label/value backgrounds + blank flow termination fix (v1.4.0-beta.1)

Label/value backgrounds:
- Labels card: Background toggle, Background Color, Background Opacity controls.
  A rounded-rectangle pill (rx=3) matching PBI's data-label style is drawn
  behind each node name label when the toggle is on.
- Values card: same three controls for node value labels (inside and outside
  positions) and ribbon value labels. Ribbon labels are now wrapped in a <g>
  per link so background + text filter together in Selection Mode: Filter.

Blank flow termination:
- After creating a link that flows INTO a null level, break the inner loop so
  no further links are generated for that row. Prevents rows like "A, null, B"
  from producing a disconnected (Blank)→B at the later levels.
  The blank-to-blank skip from v1.2.4 is retained for the null, null, B case.
